skill.name: db-migration
skill.description: Guides Alembic migration setup and schema changes for SQLModel and FastAPI projects. Use it to initialize migrations, generate revisions, apply upgrades, review rollbacks, and plan data migrations.
skill.summary: Plan, generate, apply, and review Alembic database migrations for SQLModel and FastAPI projects.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak cryptography and command execution patterns, but manual review found no malware, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration. Most high-severity cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown, migration identifiers, and validation regex. The remaining risk is operational: the skill teaches database commands and SQL migrations that can modify or remove data if used without review.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Migration Commands Require Operator Review
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Alembic upgrade and downgrade commands, including downgrade to base. These are legitimate migration operations, but they can alter or remove database schema state if run against the wrong environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and can affect live database state. The context is legitimate Alembic guidance, so this is operational risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Raw SQL Data Migration Examples Can Modify Existing Data
medium_findings.description: The examples use op.execute for data updates, enum type changes, and type drops. This is normal for migrations, but it can cause data loss or outage if copied without review, backups, and environment checks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show direct data and schema modification examples. They are relevant to the skill purpose, but they need human review before execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Database URL Placeholder in Documentation
low_findings.description: The setup examples include placeholder database URLs with user and password fields. They appear illustrative, but users should replace them with secret-managed configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a documentation example, not executable credential theft. The concern is limited to insecure copy and paste use.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches point to Markdown text, migration identifiers, regex parsing, and description handling. I found no hash, cipher, password storage, or cryptographic implementation in the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are skill metadata, migration examples, or validation regex. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use is present.

low_findings.title: Verification Script Reads Local Skill Files
low_findings.description: The verification helper accepts a skill path, reads SKILL.md, and checks frontmatter. This is expected local validation behavior and shows no network access or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script only checks local files and exits with status codes. The behavior is transparent and limited to validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database Rollback Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented downgrade commands can reverse or remove database schema changes. They should be treated as privileged operational commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clear and their effect on database state is direct. They are legitimate but potentially destructive in production.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct SQL Execution in Migration Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The migration examples execute SQL statements that update rows, alter column types, and drop enum types. These statements require testing and backup planning.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples plainly perform data and schema changes. No malicious intent is present, but the pattern has real operational risk.

content.value_statement: Database schema changes can break apps or lose data. This skill guides Alembic setup, revision generation, upgrades, rollbacks, and review checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to initialize Alembic for a project.
Shows how to configure Alembic for SQLModel metadata.
Provides commands for generating, applying, and rolling back revisions.
Gives examples for table, column, enum, and data migrations.
Lists review checks for indexes, foreign keys, defaults, and downgrade paths.
Suggests a migration directory structure for FastAPI projects.
limitations: It does not connect to a database or verify live schema state by itself.
It does not replace backups, staging tests, or production change approvals.
It focuses on Alembic, SQLModel, SQLAlchemy, and FastAPI workflows.
Generated examples must be adapted to each database dialect and project model.

use_cases.title: Set up migration tooling
use_cases.description: Initialize Alembic and configure metadata for a new SQLModel application.

use_cases.title: Review schema changes before release
use_cases.description: Check autogenerated revisions for indexes, constraints, defaults, and rollback safety.

use_cases.title: Plan safe data migrations
use_cases.description: Design batch updates and downgrade behavior for existing production data.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Alembic
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Alembic for my FastAPI and SQLModel project. Explain the files I need to create and the configuration I should review.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Revision
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a migration from model changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me generate an Alembic migration for recent SQLModel changes. Include the review checklist before I apply it.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this migration plan for data loss, missing indexes, foreign key ordering, downgrade behavior, and database dialect risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Data Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe Alembic data migration for a large table. Include batching strategy, rollback approach, testing steps, and deployment cautions.

output_examples.input: I need to add a fees table to a SQLModel project.
output_examples.output: A migration plan that creates the table, adds indexes, defines foreign keys, and lists downgrade steps for safe rollback.

output_examples.input: I changed an enum value used by existing rows.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering type conversion, existing data mapping, downgrade behavior, and staging validation before production release.

output_examples.input: I want to apply migrations during deployment.
output_examples.output: A release sequence with backup, pending revision checks, one-step upgrade guidance, smoke tests, and rollback decision points.

best_practices: Run migrations against a disposable test database before using shared environments.
Review autogenerated revisions for destructive operations and missing constraints.
Back up production data and document rollback limits before applying migrations.
anti_patterns: Applying autogenerated migrations without reading the generated operations.
Running downgrade commands against production without a recovery plan.
Embedding real database passwords in migration configuration files.

faq.question: Does this skill run database migrations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and command examples. Users must decide when and where to run migrations.

faq.question: Which migration tool does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Alembic migrations for SQLModel, SQLAlchemy, and FastAPI projects.

faq.question: Can it help with rollbacks?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains downgrade commands and patterns, but rollback safety depends on each migration.

faq.question: Can it create data migration plans?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for updating existing records and changing enum types.

faq.question: Is it safe for production databases?
faq.answer: Use it with staging tests, backups, and change review. Migration commands can change or remove production data.

faq.question: Does it support tools beyond Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
